title: The Prompt Report: A Systematic Survey of Prompt Engineering Techniques
author: Sander Schulhoff, Michael Ilie, Nishant Balepur, et al.
organization: University of Maryland, Learn Prompting, OpenAI, Stanford, Microsoft
extraction_notes: Most comprehensive prompting survey to date. 58 text-based prompting techniques, 33 vocabulary terms, 40 multimodal techniques. Taxonomy of prompt components: directive, examples, output formatting, style instructions, role. Covers ICL, thought generation, decomposition, ensembling, self-criticism. Includes agents, security, alignment. PRISMA-based systematic review of 1,565 papers.
key_claims: 58 distinct text-based prompting techniques identified through PRISMA systematic review
Prompt components include: directive, examples, output formatting, style instructions, role, additional information
Prompting is defined as the process of providing a prompt to a GenAI which then generates a response
A prompt template is a function containing variables replaced by media to create a prompt
Style instructions modify output stylistically rather than structurally
Role/persona prompting is a frequently discussed component that can improve writing and style
Structuring outputs may reduce performance on some tasks but other research shows it may improve performance
Prompt engineering is the iterative process of developing a prompt by modifying the prompting technique
Decomposition techniques include DECOMP, Faithful CoT, Least-to-Most, Plan-and-Solve, Tree-of-Thought, Skeleton-of-Thought
